results.Description: Certificate templates with CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT, authentication-capable EKU, no manager approval, and non-privileged enrollment: arbitrary SAN / impersonation risk.
results.Error: Check 'ADCS-ESC1': RootDSE has no ConfigurationNamingContext.
results.Remediation: Remove EnrolleeSuppliesSubject where not required; enable manager approval; restrict enrollment ACL to least privilege.

results.CheckName: ESC2 Any Purpose or missing EKU (certificate template)
results.Description: Templates with Any Purpose EKU or no EKU, no manager approval, enrollable by non-privileged principals — master-key / CRA abuse risk.
results.Error: Check 'ADCS-ESC2': RootDSE has no ConfigurationNamingContext.
results.Remediation: Restrict pKIExtendedKeyUsage; remove 2.5.29.37.0; enable manager approval.

results.Description: CRA template enrollable by non-privileged users and/or templates accepting CRA-signed requests — on-behalf enrollment / impersonation chain.
results.Error: Check 'ADCS-ESC3': RootDSE has no ConfigurationNamingContext.
results.Remediation: Remove CRA EKU where not required; review msPKI-RA-Application-Policies; enable manager approval.

results.Description: Non-privileged principals with write/control on enabled certificate templates — can alter template flags (e.g. ESC1 conditions).
results.Error: Check 'ADCS-ESC4': RootDSE has no ConfigurationNamingContext.
results.Remediation: Remove GenericWrite/WriteDacl/WriteOwner from non-admin principals on template objects.

results.CheckName: ESC5 Weak ACLs on critical PKI containers
results.Description: Non-privileged write access to NTAuth, Enrollment Services, AIA, CDP, OID, or PKI roots — trust chain and policy manipulation risk.
results.Error: Check 'ADCS-ESC5': RootDSE has no ConfigurationNamingContext.
results.Remediation: Restrict write to Enterprise Admins / designated PKI admins; audit object access to CN=Public Key Services.

results.Description: Enterprise CA policy may allow arbitrary SAN in requests (certutil EditFlags). Requires RPC access to CA or running on CA-adjacent host.
results.Error: Check 'ADCS-ESC6': RootDSE has no ConfigurationNamingContext.
results.Remediation: Disable EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2; apply KB5014754; validate StrongCertificateBindingEnforcement.

results.CheckName: ESC7 Weak ACLs on Enterprise CA object
results.Description: Non-privileged GenericWrite/WriteDacl on pKIEnrollmentService — maps to CA management abuse (ManageCA / officer workflows).
results.Error: Check 'ADCS-ESC7': RootDSE has no ConfigurationNamingContext.
results.Remediation: Tighten CA object ACL in AD and certsrv.msc security; align with tier model.

results.CheckName: ESC8 Web Enrollment over HTTP / relay surface
results.Description: HTTP Web Enrollment or NTLM-exposed certsrv increases relay risk; HTTPS-only still needs EPA verification.
results.Error: Check 'ADCS-ESC8': RootDSE has no ConfigurationNamingContext.
results.Remediation: Require HTTPS; enable EPA on IIS certsrv; restrict NTLM; see KB5005413.

results.Description: Informational listing: certificate template objects in the configuration partition. Use to scope PKI review; correlate with ADCS-ESC1–ESC8 and template ACL reviews. Matches alone are not proof of exploit.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Use results for inventory and change tracking. Prioritize templates with client-auth EKU and weak enrollment ACLs using the ESC checks and Microsoft PKI hardening guidance.

results.Description: Certificate templates that include client authentication EKU (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2). These are common targets for domain authentication abuse when combined with weak enrollment or subject/SAN settings.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Restrict enrollment; require manager approval where appropriate; ensure subject/SAN flags and msPKI settings align with least privilege; cross-check ADCS-ESC1–ESC3.

results.Description: Templates granting smart card logon EKU (1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.2). Mis-issued smart card logon certs can support credential abuse paths; review issuance and physical controls.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Limit enrollment; validate key archival and issuance policies; ensure smart card issuance aligns with tier model.

results.Description: Templates with PKINIT client authentication EKU (1.3.6.1.5.2.3.4). Certificates can be used in Kerberos PKINIT flows; combined with weak enrollment they raise domain authentication risk.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Tighten enrollment ACLs; review PKINIT usage; validate KDC and domain controller certificate binding settings.

results.Description: Templates allowing code signing EKU (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.3). Abuse can support malware signing or policy bypass if enrollment is too broad.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Restrict code signing template enrollment to designated groups; audit issued certificates.

results.Description: Templates without manager approval (CT_FLAG_PEND_ALL_REQUESTS not set in msPKI-Enrollment-Flag). Speeds issuance and enlarges the window for mis-issued certificates when other controls fail.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Enable manager approval for sensitive templates; combine with restricted enrollment and ADCS-ESC reviews.

results.Description: Templates with zero or missing required RA signatures (msPKI-RA-Signature). Weakens issuance workflow and can ease abuse when enrollment is misconfigured.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Require RA signatures where policy demands; align with PKI officer workflows.

results.Description: Enterprise CA enrollment service objects (pKIEnrollmentService) in AD. Lists published CAs and template bindings; use for mapping attack surface alongside ADCS-ESC6–ESC8.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Validate CA host hardening, Web Enrollment, and RPC exposure; review certificateTemplates published per CA.

results.Description: NTAuthCertificates store in AD lists CAs trusted for domain authentication. Unexpected or stale entries can affect trust decisions.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Keep NTAuth aligned with intentional enterprise roots; remove retired CAs after migration.

results.Description: Certification authority objects under the enterprise PKI tree. Broad listing helps identify roots and intermediates published to the forest.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Audit CA certificates for lifecycle and trust alignment; remove obsolete publishing.

results.Description: Templates missing pKIExpirationPeriod (or equivalent validity constraints visible in LDAP). May indicate legacy or inconsistent template definitions worth validating in CA policy.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Define and enforce validity periods per template; validate on issuing CA.

results.Description: Templates with key archival enrollment flag set. Increases sensitivity: archived keys require strict access controls on KRA and CA database.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Verify KRA configuration, ACLs, and auditing; limit templates with archival to required scenarios.

results.Description: Templates allowing exportable private keys (msPKI-Private-Key-Flag). Eases credential theft if certificates are issued to interactive users or broadly enrolled.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Disable exportable keys where not required; prefer hardware protection for high-value use cases.

results.Description: Templates with machine/enrollment name flags indicating machine certificate scenarios. Review for unintended domain computer authentication surface.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Restrict enrollment to intended device OUs and groups; validate autoenrollment GPO scope.

results.CheckName: Templates Allowing Renewal with Different Key
results.Description: Templates allowing renewal with a new key pair. Can complicate revocation and lifecycle tracking if combined with weak identity binding.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Ensure renewal policies and revocation workflows cover key-change renewals.

results.Description: User accounts with userCertificate populated. Published certs may support mapping or smart card scenarios; unexpected entries warrant review for stale or over-published credentials.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Validate intentional publication; remove obsolete certificates from the directory.

results.Description: Computer accounts with userCertificate populated. May reflect domain controller or service certs published to AD; unexpected hosts warrant validation.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Confirm published certs match intended roles; rotate and clean up stale entries.

results.Description: CRL distribution point objects in the configuration partition. Use to map revocation publication points and HTTP/S surface.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Ensure CDP URLs are reachable and HTTPS where required; monitor expiration and replication.

results.Description: Certificate template objects returned for ACL-focused review (same broad filter as inventory). Enrollment rights must be least-privilege; overly broad ACEs enable AD CS abuse—correlate with ADCS-ESC4.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Audit nTSecurityDescriptor on each template; remove Authenticated Users or Domain Users enrollment where inappropriate.

results.Description: CA objects referencing offline roots or cross-boundary trust material. Use for PKI topology documentation and continuity planning.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Validate CRL/AIA publication for offline roots; document trust chains.

results.Description: AIA container under Public Key Services. Authority Information Access publication affects chain building for clients.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Ensure AIA URLs are correct, reachable, and aligned with issuance hierarchy.

results.CheckName: Templates with Subject Name from AD
results.Description: Templates with subject name from Active Directory (CT_FLAG_SUBJECT_ALT_REQUIRE_UPN or related name flags in msPKI-Certificate-Name-Flag). Affects how subjects are bound to directory identities.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Validate name flags against intended use; cross-check with ESC1-style SAN issues.

results.Description: Template schema and minor revision fields for all certificate templates. Useful for spotting legacy v1/v2 templates still enabled.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Deprecate legacy templates; migrate to supported schema versions with explicit EKU and ACLs.

results.Description: Broad template listing flagged for vulnerability review (legacy stub name). Treat as a signal to run targeted ESC and ACL analysis—not a standalone vulnerability.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Cross-reference with ADCS-ESC1–ESC8 and per-template ACL review; retire unused templates.

results.Description: Templates selected for enrollment rights review. Enrollment and autoenrollment permissions on template objects are a primary AD CS control.
results.Error: LDAP query failed: Exception calling "FindAll" with "0" argument(s): "Unknown error (0x80005000)"
results.Remediation: Verify only intended groups may enroll; remove excessive ACEs.

results.Description: Templates in scope for client authentication correlation (legacy stub). Validate EKU and enrollment alignment with business need.

results.Remediation: Map templates to required scenarios; disable unused authentication-capable templates.

results.Description: Enterprise CA objects with timestamps for health-style review. LDAP alone does not prove outage; use with monitoring and certutil/CA logs.

results.Description: CRL distribution points with change metadata. Supports tracking revocation publication changes.

results.Description: Enterprise CA objects relevant to OCSP deployment review. LDAP does not prove OCSP responder health—follow with endpoint tests.

results.Description: Templates selected for version consistency review. Schema version drift can indicate duplicate or superseded templates still enabled.

results.Description: Templates implicated in Certificate Request Agent (CRA) abuse scenarios. Overlaps conceptually with ADCS-ESC3—LDAP highlights candidates for deeper review.

results.Description: Enterprise CA objects tied to Web Enrollment attack surface. Correlates with ADCS-ESC8 for HTTP/NTLM exposure—this LDAP check does not replace network verification.

results.Description: Templates targeted for superseded-state review. Enabled superseded templates can prolong use of weak configurations.

results.Description: Enterprise CAs for CA certificate lifetime review. Expired or soon-to-expire CA certs break chain validation.

results.Description: Templates flagged for weak key length review (legacy stub filter). Minimum key sizes must meet organizational crypto policy (e.g. RSA 2048+).

results.Description: Enterprise CAs for audit configuration review. LDAP listing does not read local CA audit policy—use CA host baselines.
